ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 (ScarCruft / Reaper) ROKRAT C2 over legitimate cloud-storage APIs
ucs.description: Hunts non-browser processes initiating outbound HTTPS to the small set of legitimate cloud storage providers (pCloud, Yandex Disk, Dropbox, Box, Mediafire) that ScarCruft's ROKRAT / DOGCALL implants have used as covert C2 channels for years. APT37 is one of the few state actors that consistently leans on these specific commodity clouds for staging and exfil, which makes the parent-process axis (any non-browser binary) a high-fidelity discriminator.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest_category=cloud_storage OR All_Traffic.dest=*api.pcloud.com OR All_Traffic.dest=*cloud-api.yandex.net OR All_Traffic.dest=*webdav.yandex.* OR All_Traffic.dest=*content.dropboxapi.com OR All_Traffic.dest=*api.dropboxapi.com OR All_Traffic.dest=*upload.box.com OR All_Traffic.dest=*api.box.com OR All_Traffic.dest=*www.mediafire.com OR All_Traffic.dest=*api.mediafire.com) by All_Traffic.src host All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.process_name _time span=1h | rename All_Traffic.* as * | search NOT process_name IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","OneDrive.exe","Dropbox.exe","Box.exe","Yandex.Disk.exe","explorer.exe","svchost.exe") | stats sum(count) as conn_count sum(bytes_out) as bytes_out values(dest) as dest values(process_name) as process_name min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by src host | where conn_count >= 5 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// APT37 ROKRAT / DOGCALL — non-browser process beaconing to its hallmark cloud-storage C2 set
let _cloud_c2_hosts = dynamic([
    "api.pcloud.com", "eapi.pcloud.com", "cloud-api.yandex.net", "webdav.yandex.com", "webdav.yandex.ru",
    "content.dropboxapi.com", "api.dropboxapi.com", "api-content.dropbox.com",
    "upload.box.com", "api.box.com", "www.mediafire.com", "api.mediafire.com"
]);
let _legit_cloud_clients = dynamic([
    "chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe",
    "onedrive.exe","dropbox.exe","box.exe","yandex.disk.exe","yandexdisk.exe",
    "teams.exe","slack.exe","outlook.exe"
]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (_cloud_c2_hosts)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_legit_cloud_clients)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            DistinctMinutes = dcount(bin(Timestamp, 1m)),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            DestHosts = make_set(RemoteUrl, 25),
            DestIPs   = make_set(RemoteIP, 25),
            SampleCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
            SampleFolder = any(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| where ConnCount >= 3                              // at least 3 calls — filters one-off probes
| join kind=leftanti (                              // first-time-seen vs 30d baseline of (host, binary, cloud)
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(30d) .. ago(7d))
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (_cloud_c2_hosts)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_legit_cloud_clients)
    | summarize by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName
  ) on DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT37 ROKRAT / DOGCALL cloud-C2 — Sentinel ASIM ImNetworkSession version
let _cloud_c2_hosts = dynamic([
    "api.pcloud.com","eapi.pcloud.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","webdav.yandex.com","webdav.yandex.ru",
    "content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","api-content.dropbox.com",
    "upload.box.com","api.box.com","www.mediafire.com","api.mediafire.com"
]);
let _legit_cloud_clients = dynamic([
    "chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe",
    "onedrive.exe","dropbox.exe","box.exe","yandex.disk.exe","yandexdisk.exe",
    "teams.exe","slack.exe","outlook.exe"
]);
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where isnotempty(Url) or isnotempty(DstHostname)
| extend Dest = coalesce(DstHostname, Url)
| where Dest has_any (_cloud_c2_hosts)
| where tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, @"\\")[-1])) !in (_legit_cloud_clients)
| where isnotempty(ActingProcessName)
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            DestHosts = make_set(Dest, 25),
            SampleCmd = any(ActingProcessCommandLine)
            by DvcHostname, ActingProcessName, ActorUsername
| where ConnCount >= 3
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT37 ROKRAT Cloud-Storage C2 (Non-Browser Egress to pCloud/Yandex/Dropbox/Box/Mediafire)
id: 6b1f1e2a-7d6c-4a35-93ad-37e4c1c1f111
status: experimental
description: Detects non-browser, non-sync-client processes contacting the legitimate cloud-storage APIs that APT37/ScarCruft's ROKRAT and DOGCALL implants have repeatedly used as bidirectional C2 channels.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0067/
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/rokrat-reloaded/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.exfiltration
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.g0067
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_dest:
    DestinationHostname:
      - 'api.pcloud.com'
      - 'eapi.pcloud.com'
      - 'cloud-api.yandex.net'
      - 'webdav.yandex.com'
      - 'webdav.yandex.ru'
      - 'content.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'api.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'api-content.dropbox.com'
      - 'upload.box.com'
      - 'api.box.com'
      - 'www.mediafire.com'
      - 'api.mediafire.com'
  filter_browsers:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\brave.exe'
      - '\iexplore.exe'
      - '\opera.exe'
      - '\OneDrive.exe'
      - '\Dropbox.exe'
      - '\Box.exe'
      - '\Yandex.Disk.exe'
      - '\YandexDisk.exe'
      - '\Teams.exe'
      - '\slack.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
  condition: selection_dest and not filter_browsers
falsepositives:
  - Custom integrations or backup utilities written in-house that legitimately reach cloud storage APIs (allowlist by SHA256/path)
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT37/ScarCruft (a.k.a. Reaper, InkySquid, Ricochet Chollima, Group123, TEMP.Reaper) is the most prolific state actor pinned to ROKRAT/DOGCALL/CORALDECK — implants whose published reports (Talos, Securelist, Volexity) all centre on the same narrow cloud-storage C2 set: pCloud, Yandex Disk, Dropbox, Box and Mediafire. Other DPRK clusters (Lazarus, Kimsuky) more commonly use compromised web servers

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActorUsername` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImAuthentication, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.

ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 HWP/LNK lure → mshta or PowerShell child of Hangul / Office / Explorer
ucs.description: Hunts the ScarCruft execution chain that follows opening a weaponised Hangul (HWP/HWPX) document or LNK lure: the Hangul Word Processor (Hwp.exe / Hwp64.exe / GBB.exe), Office, or Explorer (LNK click) spawning mshta.exe, wscript.exe/JScript, or PowerShell that pulls a stage-2 from the internet. APT37 is uniquely identifiable by the HWP vector targeting South Korean victims combined with mshta/JS staging.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as child_path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("Hwp.exe","Hwp64.exe","GBB.exe","Gbb.exe","WINWORD.EXE","EXCEL.EXE","POWERPNT.EXE","explorer.exe","OUTLOOK.EXE")) (Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cmd.exe")) NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.user Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | rename Processes.* as * | search (cmdline="*http*" OR cmdline="*hxxp*" OR cmdline="*FromBase64String*" OR cmdline="*EncodedCommand*" OR cmdline="*-enc *" OR cmdline="*DownloadString*" OR cmdline="*DownloadFile*" OR cmdline="*IEX*" OR cmdline="*Invoke-Expression*" OR cmdline="*.hta*" OR cmdline="*WScript.Shell*" OR cmdline="*MSXML2.XMLHTTP*" OR cmdline="*\\\\Temp\\\\*" OR cmdline="*\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\Temp\\\\*") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft — Hangul/Office/LNK weaponized-doc execution chain
let _doc_parents = dynamic([
    "hwp.exe","hwp64.exe","gbb.exe",                        // Hangul Word Processor
    "winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe",
    "explorer.exe",                                          // LNK double-click
    "outlook.exe"
]);
let _suspicious_children = dynamic([
    "mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe",
    "powershell.exe","pwsh.exe",
    "rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cmd.exe"
]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_doc_parents)
| where FileName in~ (_suspicious_children)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (
      "http://","https://","hxxp",".hta",
      "FromBase64String","-EncodedCommand","-enc ","-ec ",
      "DownloadString","DownloadFile","IEX","Invoke-Expression",
      "WScript.Shell","MSXML2.XMLHTTP","BitsTransfer",
      @"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Public\"
  )
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
          IsRemote    = InitiatingProcessAccountObjectId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft Hangul-doc / LNK execution chain — Sentinel via 4688
let _doc_parents = dynamic([
    @"\hwp.exe",@"\hwp64.exe",@"\gbb.exe",
    @"\winword.exe",@"\excel.exe",@"\powerpnt.exe",
    @"\explorer.exe",@"\outlook.exe"
]);
let _suspicious_children = dynamic([
    @"\mshta.exe",@"\wscript.exe",@"\cscript.exe",
    @"\powershell.exe",@"\pwsh.exe",
    @"\rundll32.exe",@"\regsvr32.exe",@"\cmd.exe"
]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLower = tolower(ParentProcessName), ChildLower = tolower(NewProcessName)
| where ParentLower has_any (_doc_parents)
| where ChildLower has_any (_suspicious_children)
| where CommandLine has_any (
      "http://","https://","hxxp",".hta",
      "FromBase64String","-EncodedCommand","-enc ","-ec ",
      "DownloadString","DownloadFile","IEX","Invoke-Expression",
      "WScript.Shell","MSXML2.XMLHTTP","BitsTransfer",
      @"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Public\"
  )
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT37 ScarCruft Hangul/Office/LNK Lure Spawning Script Interpreter with Network or Encoded Payload
id: 8e3a6fe2-4711-4d50-9b73-7c3a9d2f4d22
status: experimental
description: Detects the post-exploitation chain typical of APT37 (ScarCruft / Reaper / InkySquid) where a Hangul Word Processor, Office, Explorer (LNK click) or Outlook process spawns mshta, wscript/cscript, PowerShell, rundll32, regsvr32, or cmd with a download/decode/temp-staging command line.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0067/
  - https://securelist.com/scarcruft-continues-to-evolve/
  - https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2018/02/apt37-overlooked-north-korean-actor.html
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.005
  - attack.t1059.007
  - attack.g0067
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\Hwp.exe'
      - '\Hwp64.exe'
      - '\GBB.exe'
      - '\WINWORD.EXE'
      - '\EXCEL.EXE'
      - '\POWERPNT.EXE'
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\OUTLOOK.EXE'
  child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
  payload_indicators:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
      - 'hxxp'
      - '.hta'
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - '-enc '
      - '-ec '
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'DownloadFile'
      - 'IEX'
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
      - 'WScript.Shell'
      - 'MSXML2.XMLHTTP'
      - 'BitsTransfer'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\Public\'
  condition: parent and child and payload_indicators
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate macros / templates that fetch content from intranet hosts (allowlist by author or path)
  - Help-desk troubleshooting scripts launched from Outlook attachments
level: high
ucs.rationale: ScarCruft / APT37 is the only widely-tracked APT whose initial-access tradecraft consistently leverages Hangul Word Processor (HWP/HWPX) lures targeting South Korean / DPRK-defector / journalist verticals (FireEye 2018 'overlooked actor', Securelist 'ScarCruft continues to evolve', Volexity InkySquid). Pairing a Hangul or Office parent with a script-host child plus a network/decode command-line un

_field_issues.message: `FromBase64String` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`EncodedCommand` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`DownloadString` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`DownloadFile` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`IEX` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Invoke` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Expression` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`WScript` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`MSXML2` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`BitsTransfer` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Temp` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `Timestamp`?
`AppData` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Local` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Public` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

`EncodedCommand` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`DownloadString` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.

`Invoke` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
